topic: Superpowers Framework: TDD Methodology for AI Coding Agents 2026

key_points: Comprehensive structured guide to Superpowers framework for AI coding agents
Explains 5-phase discipline: clarify → design → plan → code → verify
Covers all 14 skills included in the framework
Explains how TDD enforcement differs from simple prompt templates
Original blog post by Jesse Vincent (obra), the creator of Superpowers
Explains motivation: AI agents skip best practices when in a hurry
Describes the brainstorming-first workflow and git worktree isolation
Personal account of using coding agents with the framework
Beginner-friendly explanation of the Superpowers agent skills framework
Covers installation for Claude Code, Cursor, Codex CLI, and OpenCode
Describes how skills auto-trigger based on context
Explains YAGNI and DRY principles enforced by the framework
Discusses the explosive growth: 89K+ GitHub stars by March 2026
Compares Superpowers to simple .cursorrules and CLAUDE.md approaches
Explains SKILL.md file architecture and how skills compose
Covers Superpowers 5 update: visual brainstorming with HTML mockups
Professional workflow in practice with AI coding agents using Superpowers
Covers subagent-driven development with two-stage code review
Explains how skills dispatch fresh subagents per task
Real-world examples of enforced TDD producing production-ready code

stats.fact: Superpowers has reached 129,443 GitHub stars as of April 2026, among the highest of any coding-agent repository
Superpowers gained 1,528 stars within the first 24 hours of its October 2025 launch
Developers using Superpowers report 85-95% test coverage, compared to 30-50% with standard Claude Code prompting
The chardet project shipped version 7.0.0 using Superpowers TDD: 41x faster, 96.8% accuracy, test suite covering 2,161 files across 99 encodings
Spec-driven development (writing specs before prompting the agent) reduces rollback rates by 60%+ compared to vague prompts
AI coding tools deliver an average 3-5 hours/week time savings (top quartile: 5-8 hours), with healthy ROI at 2.5-3.5x
Without TDD, AI agents write implementation code then generate tests that verify the code they just wrote — tests pass by construction but don't verify actual requirements

angles: Why AI agents fail without TDD — the 'tests-that-pass-by-construction' problem explained with code examples

The 5-phase discipline (clarify → design → plan → code → verify) and why it beats raw prompting
